method.name: version_number
method.description: The SQLITE_VERSION_NUMBER C preprocessor macro resolves to an integer with the value (X*1000000 + Y*1000 + Z) where X, Y, and Z are the same numbers used in SQLITE_VERSION.

method.name: version
method.description: The SQLITE_VERSION C preprocessor macro in the sqlite3.h header evaluates to a string literal that is the SQLite version in the format "X.Y.Z" where X is the major version number (always 3 for SQLite3) and Y is the minor version number and Z is the release number.

method.name: get_sqlite3_version
method.description: The sqlite3_version[] string constant contains the text of the SQLITE_VERSION macro.

method.name: sqlite3_libversion
method.description: The sqlite3_libversion() function returns a pointer to the sqlite3_version[] string constant.

method.name: sqlite3_sourceid
method.description: The sqlite3_sourceid() function returns a pointer to a string constant whose value is the same as the SQLITE_SOURCE_ID C preprocessor macro.

method.name: sqlite3_libversion_number
method.description: The sqlite3_libversion_number() function returns an integer equal to SQLITE_VERSION_NUMBER.

method.name: compileoption_used
method.description: Returns 0 or 1 indicating whether the specified option was defined at compile time. The SQLITE_ prefix may be omitted from the option name passed to this function.

method.name: compileoption_get
method.description: Returns the N-th compile time option string. If N is out of range, returns an empty String. The SQLITE_ prefix is omitted from any strings returned by this function.

method.name: sqlite3_threadsafe
method.description: Returns zero if and only if SQLite was compiled with mutexing code omitted due to the SQLITE_THREADSAFE compile-time option being set to 0.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes a database connection. Returns SQLITE_OK if the sqlite3 object is successfully destroyed and all associated resources are deallocated.

method.name: ok
method.description: Successful result

method.name: error
method.description: Generic error

method.name: internal
method.description: Internal logic error in SQLite

method.name: perm
method.description: Access permission denied

method.name: abort
method.description: Callback routine requested an abort

method.name: busy
method.description: The database file is locked

method.name: locked
method.description: A table in the database is locked

method.name: nomem
method.description: A malloc() failed

method.name: readonly
method.description: Attempt to write a readonly database

method.name: interrupt
method.description: Operation terminated by sqlite3_interrupt()

method.name: ioerr
method.description: Some kind of disk I/O error occurred

method.name: corrupt
method.description: The database disk image is malformed

method.name: notfound
method.description: Unknown opcode in sqlite3_file_control()

method.name: full
method.description: Insertion failed because database is full

method.name: cantopen
method.description: Unable to open the database file

method.name: protocol
method.description: Database lock protocol error

method.name: empty
method.description: Internal use only

method.name: schema
method.description: The database schema changed

method.name: toobig
method.description: String or BLOB exceeds size limit

method.name: constraint
method.description: Abort due to constraint violation

method.name: mismatch
method.description: Data type mismatch

method.name: misuse
method.description: Library used incorrectly

method.name: nolfs
method.description: Uses OS features not supported on host

method.name: auth
method.description: Authorization denied

method.name: format
method.description: Not used

method.name: range
method.description: 2nd parameter to sqlite3_bind out of range

method.name: notadb
method.description: File opened that is not a database file

method.name: notice
method.description: Notifications from sqlite3_log()

method.name: warning
method.description: Warnings from sqlite3_log()

method.name: row
method.description: sqlite3_step() has another row ready

method.name: done
method.description: sqlite3_step() has finished executing

method.name: open_readonly
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_readwrite
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_create
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_memory
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_uri
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_nomutex
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_fullmutex
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_sharedcache
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_privatecache
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: open_nofollow
method.description: Ok for sqlite3_open_v2()

method.name: fcntl_lockstate
method.description: Used for debugging. Writes the current state of the lock into an integer that the pArg argument points to.

method.name: fcntl_get_lockproxyfile
method.description: Used to get the proxy file for file locking.

method.name: fcntl_set_lockproxyfile
method.description: Used to set the proxy file for file locking.

method.name: fcntl_last_errno
method.description: Returns the last errno.

method.name: fcntl_size_hint
method.description: Used by SQLite to give the VFS layer a hint of how large the database file will grow.

method.name: fcntl_chunk_size
method.description: Used to request that the VFS extends and truncates the database file in chunks.

method.name: fcntl_file_pointer
method.description: Obtains a pointer to the sqlite3_file object associated with a particular database connection.

method.name: fcntl_sync_omitted
method.description: No longer used.

method.name: fcntl_win32_av_retry
method.description: Used to configure automatic retry counts and intervals for disk I/O operations on Windows.

method.name: fcntl_persist_wal
method.description: Sets or queries the persistent WAL setting.

method.name: fcntl_overwrite
method.description: Indicates that the entire database file will be overwritten by the current transaction.

method.name: fcntl_vfsname
method.description: Obtains the names of all VFSes in the VFS stack.

method.name: fcntl_powersafe_overwrite
method.description: Sets or queries the persistent powersafe-overwrite setting.

method.name: fcntl_pragma
method.description: Sent to the VFS when a PRAGMA statement is parsed.

method.name: fcntl_busyhandler
method.description: Provides access to the connection's busy-handler callback.

method.name: fcntl_tempfilename
method.description: Generates a temporary filename using the same algorithm as for TEMP tables.

method.name: fcntl_mmap_size
method.description: Queries or sets the maximum number of bytes for memory-mapped I/O.

method.name: fcntl_trace
method.description: Provides advisory information to the VFS about what the higher layers are doing.

method.name: fcntl_has_moved
method.description: Indicates whether the file has been renamed, moved, or deleted since it was first opened.

method.name: fcntl_sync
method.description: Sent to the VFS immediately before the xSync method is invoked.

method.name: fcntl_commit_phasetwo
method.description: Sent to the VFS after a transaction has been committed but before the database is unlocked.

method.name: fcntl_win32_set_handle
method.description: Used for debugging. Swaps the file handle with the one pointed to by the pArg argument.

method.name: fcntl_wal_block
method.description: Signals that it might be advantageous to block on the next WAL lock.

method.name: fcntl_zipvfs
method.description: Implemented by zipvfs only. All other VFS should return SQLITE_NOTFOUND.

method.name: fcntl_rbu
method.description: Implemented by the special VFS used by the RBU extension only.

method.name: fcntl_vfs_pointer
method.description: Finds a pointer to the top-level VFSes currently in use.

method.name: fcntl_journal_pointer
method.description: Obtains a pointer to the journal file associated with the database connection.

method.name: fcntl_win32_get_handle
method.description: Obtains the underlying native file handle associated with a file handle.

method.name: fcntl_pdb
method.description: Internal use only.

method.name: fcntl_begin_atomic_write
method.description: Places the file descriptor in batch write mode.

method.name: fcntl_commit_atomic_write
method.description: Causes all write operations since the previous BEGIN_ATOMIC_WRITE to be performed atomically.

method.name: fcntl_rollback_atomic_write
method.description: Causes all write operations since the previous BEGIN_ATOMIC_WRITE to be rolled back.

method.name: fcntl_lock_timeout
method.description: Configures a VFS to block for up to M milliseconds before failing when attempting to obtain a file lock.

method.name: fcntl_data_version
method.description: Used to detect changes to a database file.

method.name: fcntl_size_limit
method.description: Used by in-memory VFS to set an upper bound on the size of the in-memory database.

method.name: fcntl_ckpt_done
method.description: Invoked from within a checkpoint after copying pages from the WAL file.

method.name: fcntl_reserve_bytes
method.description: Internal use only.

method.name: fcntl_ckpt_start
method.description: Invoked from within a checkpoint before copying pages from the WAL file.

method.name: fcntl_external_reader
method.description: Detects whether there is a database client in another process with a wal-mode transaction open.

method.name: fcntl_cks_file
method.description: For use internally by the checksum VFS shim only.

method.name: fcntl_reset_cache
method.description: Purges the contents of the in-memory page cache.

method.name: fcntl_null_io
method.description: Sets the low-level file descriptor such that it will no longer read or write to the database file.

method.name: fcntl_block_on_connect
method.description: Configures the VFS to block when taking a SHARED lock to connect to a wal mode database.

method.name: fcntl_filestat
method.description: Returns low-level diagnostic information about the sqlite3_file objects.

method.name: initialize
method.description: Initializes the SQLite library. This routine is designed to aid in process initialization and shutdown on embedded systems. Workstation applications using SQLite normally do not need to invoke this routine as it is called automatically by other SQLite interfaces.

method.name: shutdown
method.description: Deallocates any resources that were allocated by sqlite3_initialize(). This routine is designed to aid in process initialization and shutdown on embedded systems. Workstation applications using SQLite normally do not need to invoke this routine. All open database connections must be closed and all other SQLite resources must be deallocated prior to invoking this routine.

method.name: os_init
method.description: Performs operating-system specific initialization of the SQLite library. This routine is called automatically by sqlite3_initialize(). Typical tasks include allocation or deallocation of static resources, initialization of global variables, setting up a default sqlite3_vfs module, or setting up a default configuration using sqlite3_config().

method.name: os_end
method.description: Undoes the effect of sqlite3_os_init(). This routine is invoked indirectly by sqlite3_shutdown(). Typical tasks include deallocation of static resources, cleanup of global variables, and tearing down the default sqlite3_vfs module.

method.name: access_exists
method.description: Checks whether the file exists.

method.name: access_readwrite
method.description: Checks whether the named directory is both readable and writable. Used by PRAGMA temp_store_directory.

method.name: access_read
method.description: Checks whether the file is readable. Currently unused.

method.name: shm_unlock
method.description: Used in xShmLock to release a lock.

method.name: shm_lock
method.description: Used in xShmLock to acquire a lock.

method.name: shm_shared
method.description: Used in xShmLock to specify shared lock.

method.name: shm_exclusive
method.description: Used in xShmLock to specify exclusive lock.

method.name: shm_nlock
method.description: Maximum xShmLock index. The xShmLock method can use values between 0 and this upper bound as its offset argument.

method.name: total_changes64
method.description: Returns the total number of rows inserted, modified or deleted by all INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE statements completed since the database connection was opened as a 64-bit integer, avoiding overflow issues.

method.name: config_singlethread
method.description: Sets the threading mode to Single-thread. Disables all mutexing.

method.name: config_multithread
method.description: Sets the threading mode to Multi-thread. Disables mutexing on database connection and prepared statement objects.

method.name: config_serialized
method.description: Sets the threading mode to Serialized. Enables all mutexes including recursive mutexes on database connection and prepared statement objects.

method.name: config_malloc
method.description: Takes a pointer to an sqlite3_mem_methods structure to specify alternative memory allocation routines.

method.name: config_getmalloc
method.description: Takes a pointer to an sqlite3_mem_methods structure to fill with the currently defined memory allocation routines.

method.name: config_scratch
method.description: No longer used.

method.name: config_pagecache
method.description: Specifies a memory pool that SQLite can use for the database page cache.

method.name: config_heap
method.description: Specifies a static memory buffer that SQLite will use for all dynamic memory allocation needs beyond those provided for by SQLITE_CONFIG_PAGECACHE.

method.name: config_memstatus
method.description: Takes a boolean argument to enable or disable the collection of memory allocation statistics.

method.name: config_mutex
method.description: Takes a pointer to an sqlite3_mutex_methods structure to specify alternative low-level mutex routines.

method.name: config_getmutex
method.description: Takes a pointer to an sqlite3_mutex_methods structure to fill with the currently defined mutex routines.

method.name: config_lookaside
method.description: Takes two arguments: the size of each lookaside buffer slot and the number of slots allocated to each database connection.

method.name: config_pcache
method.description: No-op. Obsolete.

method.name: config_getpcache
method.description: No-op. Obsolete.

method.name: config_log
method.description: Used to configure the SQLite global error log. Takes a function pointer and a void pointer.

method.name: config_uri
method.description: Takes a boolean argument to globally enable or disable URI handling.

method.name: config_pcache2
method.description: Takes a pointer to an sqlite3_pcache_methods2 object to specify a custom page cache implementation.

method.name: config_getpcache2
method.description: Takes a pointer to an sqlite3_pcache_methods2 object to copy the current page cache implementation into.

method.name: config_covering_index_scan
method.description: Takes a boolean argument to enable or disable the use of covering indices for full table scans.

method.name: config_sqllog
method.description: Takes a function pointer and a void pointer to configure SQL logging.

method.name: config_mmap_size
method.description: Takes two 64-bit integers for the default mmap size limit and the maximum allowed mmap size limit.

method.name: config_win32_heapsize
method.description: Takes a 32-bit unsigned integer specifying the maximum size of the created heap (Windows only).

method.name: config_pcache_hdrsz
method.description: Takes a pointer to an integer and writes the number of extra bytes per page required for each page in SQLITE_CONFIG_PAGECACHE.

method.name: config_pmasz
method.description: Takes an unsigned integer to set the Minimum PMA Size for the multithreaded sorter.

method.name: config_stmtjrnl_spill
method.description: Takes an integer to set the statement journal spill-to-disk threshold.

method.name: config_small_malloc
method.description: Takes a boolean hint to avoid large memory allocations if possible.

method.name: config_sorterref_size
method.description: Takes an integer to set the sorter-reference size threshold.

method.name: config_memdb_maxsize
method.description: Takes a sqlite3_int64 to set the default maximum size for an in-memory database created using sqlite3_deserialize().

method.name: config_rowid_in_view
method.description: Takes a pointer to an integer to enable or disable the ability for VIEWs to have a ROWID.

method.name: dbconfig_maindbname
method.description: Used to change the name of the "main" database schema.

method.name: dbconfig_lookaside
method.description: Used to adjust the configuration of the lookaside memory allocator within a database connection.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_fkey
method.description: Used to enable or disable the enforcement of foreign key constraints.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_trigger
method.description: Used to enable or disable triggers.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_fts3_tokenizer
method.description: Used to enable or disable using the fts3_tokenizer() function without using bound parameters.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_load_extension
method.description: Used to enable or disable the sqlite3_load_extension() interface independently of the load_extension() SQL function.

method.name: dbconfig_no_ckpt_on_close
method.description: Used to override the default checkpoint behavior when a WAL mode database is closed.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_qpsg
method.description: Activates or deactivates the query planner stability guarantee (QPSG).

method.name: dbconfig_trigger_eqp
method.description: Controls whether EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output includes operations performed by trigger programs.

method.name: dbconfig_reset_database
method.description: Used to reset a database back to an empty database with no schema and no content.

method.name: dbconfig_defensive
method.description: Activates or deactivates the "defensive" flag for a database connection, disabling features that could deliberately corrupt the database.

method.name: dbconfig_writable_schema
method.description: Activates or deactivates the "writable_schema" flag, equivalent to PRAGMA writable_schema.

method.name: dbconfig_legacy_alter_table
method.description: Activates or deactivates the legacy behavior of the ALTER TABLE RENAME command.

method.name: dbconfig_dqs_dml
method.description: Activates or deactivates the legacy double-quoted string literal misfeature for DML statements.

method.name: dbconfig_dqs_ddl
method.description: Activates or deactivates the legacy double-quoted string literal misfeature for DDL statements.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_view
method.description: Used to enable or disable views.

method.name: dbconfig_legacy_file_format
method.description: Activates or deactivates the legacy file format flag, causing newly created database files to have schema format version 1.

method.name: dbconfig_trusted_schema
method.description: Tells SQLite to assume that database schemas are untainted by malicious content.

method.name: dbconfig_stmt_scanstatus
method.description: Sets or clears a flag that enables collection of run-time performance statistics used by sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus_v2().

method.name: dbconfig_reverse_scanorder
method.description: Changes the default order in which tables and indexes are scanned so that the scans start at the end and work toward the beginning.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_attach_create
method.description: Enables or disables the ability of the ATTACH DATABASE SQL command to create a new database file if the database file does not already exist.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_attach_write
method.description: Enables or disables the ability of the ATTACH DATABASE SQL command to open a database for writing.

method.name: dbconfig_enable_comments
method.description: Enables or disables the ability to include comments in SQL text.

method.name: dbconfig_fp_digits
method.description: Determines the number of significant digits that SQLite will attempt to preserve when converting floating point numbers into text.

method.name: dbconfig_max
method.description: The largest DBCONFIG option value.

method.name: extended_result_codes
method.description: Enables or disables the extended result codes feature of SQLite. Extended result codes are disabled by default for historical compatibility.

method.name: changes64
method.description: Returns the number of rows modified, inserted or deleted by the most recently completed INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE statement as a 64-bit integer, avoiding overflow issues.

method.name: complete16
method.description: UTF-16 version of complete(). Determines if an SQL statement is complete. Input must be a zero-terminated UTF-16 string in native byte order.

method.name: malloc64
method.description: Allocates at least size bytes of memory using 64-bit size. Returns a pointer to the allocated memory as an int64_t, or 0 if size == 0 or allocation fails. Memory must be freed with sqlite3_free().

method.name: busy_handler
method.description: Sets a callback to handle SQLITE_BUSY errors. The callback receives the number of previous invocations as an argument and returns 0 to stop retrying or non-zero to retry.

method.name: free_table
method.description: Frees memory allocated by sqlite3_get_table(). Must only be called with a result pointer obtained from sqlite3_get_table().

method.name: is_interrupted
method.description: Returns true if an interrupt is currently in effect for the database connection.

method.name: free_ptr
method.description: Frees memory allocated by SQLite's memory allocation subsystem (e.g., sqlite3_malloc, sqlite3_realloc). Passing NULL is safe and results in a no-op. Only pointers obtained from SQLite's allocation functions should be passed.

method.name: memory_highwater
method.description: Returns the maximum value of memory used since the high-water mark was last reset. If reset is true, resets the high-water mark to the current memory usage and returns the previous high-water mark.

method.name: realloc
method.description: Resizes a prior memory allocation to at least size bytes. If ptr is 0, behaves like sqlite3_malloc(size). If size <= 0, behaves like sqlite3_free(ptr). Returns 0 on failure or if size <= 0.

method.name: realloc64
method.description: Resizes a prior memory allocation to at least size bytes using 64-bit size. If ptr is 0, behaves like sqlite3_malloc64(size). If size is 0, behaves like sqlite3_free(ptr). Returns 0 on failure or if size == 0.

method.name: malloc
method.description: Allocates at least size bytes of memory. Returns a pointer to the allocated memory, or 0 if size <= 0 or allocation fails. Memory must be freed with sqlite3_free().

method.name: memory_used
method.description: Returns the number of bytes of memory currently outstanding (malloced but not freed) by SQLite's memory subsystem.

method.name: msize
method.description: Returns the size (in bytes) of a memory block previously allocated by SQLite's memory subsystem. Returns 0 if ptr is NULL. Only pointers obtained from SQLite's allocation functions should be passed.

method.name: randomness
method.description: Fills a buffer with pseudo-random bytes using SQLite's PRNG. The buffer pointer can be NULL, in which case only seeding occurs. The length parameter specifies the number of bytes to generate.

method.name: trace
method.description: DEPRECATED: Registers a trace callback for SQL statement execution. The callback receives the SQL statement text as a String. Use sqlite3_trace_v2() instead for new code.

method.name: set_authorizer
method.description: Registers an authorizer callback for SQL statement compilation. The callback receives action code and four optional string arguments. Return SQLITE_OK to allow, SQLITE_IGNORE to skip the action but continue compilation, or SQLITE_DENY to reject the statement.

method.name: trace_v2
method.description: Registers a trace callback for SQL statement events. The callback receives: event type (int), context (int64_t), and two int64_t arguments (P and X) whose meanings depend on the event type (see SQLITE_TRACE_STMT, etc.). Returns 0 on success. The callback should return 0 for future compatibility.

method.name: uri_parameter
method.description: Returns the value of a URI query parameter from a database filename. If the parameter is not found, returns an empty String. If the parameter exists but has no value, returns an empty String (not null). The filename must be a valid SQLite filename (e.g., obtained from SQLite core).

method.name: uri_boolean
method.description: Returns the boolean interpretation of a URI query parameter. Returns true if the parameter value is 'yes', 'true', 'on' (case-insensitive) or starts with a non-zero digit; false if 'no', 'false', 'off' or starts with '0'; otherwise returns default_value. If the parameter is missing, returns default_value.

method.name: uri_int64
method.description: Returns the 64-bit integer value of a URI query parameter. If the parameter is missing or not a valid integer, returns default_value.

method.name: uri_key
method.description: Returns the name (key) of the N-th URI query parameter (zero-based index). Returns an empty String if the index is out of range (negative or beyond the number of parameters).

method.name: filename_database
method.description: Returns the database filename corresponding to a given SQLite filename (database, journal, or WAL). The input must be a filename obtained from SQLite core (e.g., passed to VFS xOpen or from sqlite3_db_filename()). Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

method.name: errmsg
method.description: Returns the English-language error message (UTF-8) for the most recent error on the database connection. Returns an empty String if no error message is available. The message is managed internally and may be overwritten by subsequent API calls.

method.name: filename_wal
method.description: Returns the WAL (Write-Ahead Log) filename corresponding to a given SQLite filename (database, journal, or WAL). The input must be a filename obtained from SQLite core (e.g., passed to VFS xOpen or from sqlite3_db_filename()). Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

method.name: database_file_object
method.description: Returns the sqlite3_file* handle for the main database file corresponding to a journal/WAL filename. This is intended for use in custom VFS implementations only. The input must be a filename passed to xOpen with SQLITE_OPEN_MAIN_JOURNAL or SQLITE_OPEN_WAL flags. Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

method.name: create_filename
method.description: ⚠️ VFS-only function. Creates a filename object from database, journal, WAL, and URI parameters. The returned pointer must be freed with sqlite3_free_filename(). The input strings must remain valid until then. In Godot, ensure strings are not temporary (e.g., use static strings or manage lifetime carefully).

method.name: free_filename
method.description: Frees memory allocated by sqlite3_create_filename. Must only be called on pointers returned by create_filename. Passing NULL or invalid pointers causes undefined behavior.

method.name: filename_journal
method.description: Returns the rollback journal filename corresponding to a given SQLite filename (database, journal, or WAL). The input must be a filename obtained from SQLite core (e.g., passed to VFS xOpen or from sqlite3_db_filename()). Behavior is undefined for invalid inputs.

method.name: extended_errcode
method.description: Returns the extended result code for the most recent API call on the database connection. Unlike errcode(), this always returns the extended code, even if extended codes are disabled globally.

method.name: errcode
method.description: Returns the numeric result code for the most recent API call on the database connection. Returns SQLITE_OK if no error occurred. The error code may change with subsequent API calls.

method.name: errstr
method.description: Returns the English-language error message (UTF-8) for a given SQLite result code. Returns an empty String if the result code is not recognized.

method.name: limit
method.description: Sets or queries a run-time limit for the database connection. If new_value is negative, the limit is unchanged (query mode). Returns the prior limit value. Attempts to exceed compile-time hard upper bounds are silently truncated.

method.name: get_expanded_sql
method.description: Returns the UTF-8 SQL text of the prepared statement with bound parameters expanded. The returned string is allocated by SQLite and must be freed by the application. For example, if a parameter is bound to 2345, it will show "2345" instead of "$abc".

method.name: bind_blob
method.description: Binds a BLOB value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If data is empty, binds NULL instead. The data is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

method.name: bind_blob64
method.description: Binds a BLOB value to a parameter in the prepared statement using 64-bit size. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If data is empty, binds NULL instead. The data is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

method.name: bind_int64
method.description: Binds a 64-bit integer value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: bind_zeroblob
method.description: Binds a zero-filled BLOB of specified length to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. A negative length results in a zero-length BLOB.

method.name: column_name16
method.description: Returns the name assigned to a particular column in the result set as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_database_name16
method.description: Returns the database name that is the origin of a particular result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_table_name
method.description: Returns the table name that is the origin of a particular result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_decltype16
method.description: Returns the declared datatype of a query result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the column is an expression or subquery.

method.name: constant_text
method.description: String type code.

method.name: column_blob
method.description: Returns a BLOB result from a query as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty PackedByteArray if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_double
method.description: Returns a REAL (floating-point) result from a query. Returns 0.0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_int
method.description: Returns a 32-bit INTEGER result from a query. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_int64
method.description: Returns a 64-bit INTEGER result from a query. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: value_blob
method.description: Extracts a BLOB value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty PackedByteArray if the value is NULL or if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_double
method.description: Extracts a REAL (floating-point) value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0.0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_int
method.description: Extracts a 32-bit INTEGER value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_int64
method.description: Extracts a 64-bit INTEGER value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_pointer
method.description: Extracts a pointer value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid or if the pointer type doesn't match.

method.name: value_bytes
method.description: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-8 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_free
method.description: Frees an sqlite3_value object previously obtained from sqlite3_value_dup(). If value is NULL then this function is a harmless no-op.

method.name: set_auxdata
method.description: Sets auxiliary data for the Nth argument of the application-defined function. Uses NULL as the destructor (no cleanup).

method.name: set_clientdata
method.description: Sets client data associated with the database connection and name. Uses NULL as the destructor (no cleanup). Returns 0 on success, SQLITE_NOMEM on allocation failure.

method.name: constant_sqlite_static
method.description: Special destructor value indicating the content pointer is constant and will never change. No destruction needed.

method.name: constant_sqlite_transient
method.description: Special destructor value indicating the content will likely change and SQLite should make its own private copy.

method.name: result_blob
method.description: Sets the result from an application-defined function to be the BLOB specified by the value. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

method.name: result_blob64
method.description: Sets the result from an application-defined function to be the BLOB specified by the value. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

method.name: result_double
method.description: Sets the result from an application-defined function to be the floating point value specified.

method.name: result_error16
method.description: Causes the implemented SQL function to throw an error with the specified UTF-16 message. Uses -1 for the length parameter to let SQLite search for the null terminator.

method.name: result_error_nomem
method.description: Causes SQLite to throw an error indicating that a memory allocation failed.

method.name: result_value
method.description: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a copy of the sqlite3_value object. The input value can be protected or unprotected.

method.name: create_collation_v2
method.description: Adds a new collation to the database connection with the ability to specify a destructor callback. The destructor parameter is omitted (uses nullptr). The compare_func callback receives two String arguments and must return an integer that is negative, zero, or positive if the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second, respectively.

method.name: collation_needed16
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever an undefined collation sequence is required. The callback receives three arguments: a Ref<Sqlite3Handle> for the database connection, an integer for the text representation, and a String for the collation name (UTF-16 encoded).

method.name: set_temp_directory
method.description: Sets the temporary directory path. The path is converted to UTF-8 and stored using sqlite3_mprintf. Note: This function should be called before opening any database connections and only from a single thread.

method.name: get_temp_directory
method.description: Gets the current temporary directory path. Returns an empty String if the temporary directory is not set.

method.name: set_data_directory
method.description: Sets the data directory path for database files. Only used by the Windows VFS. The path is converted to UTF-8 and stored using sqlite3_mprintf. Note: This function should be called before opening any database connections and only from a single thread.

method.name: get_data_directory
method.description: Gets the current data directory path. Returns an empty String if the data directory is not set. Only used by the Windows VFS.

method.name: win32_set_directory
method.description: Sets the value associated with sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory. Pass an empty String to free the previous value. This function is Windows-specific.

method.name: win32_set_directory8
method.description: Sets the value associated with sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory using a UTF-8 string. Pass an empty String to free the previous value. This function is Windows-specific.

method.name: win32_set_directory16
method.description: Sets the value associated with sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory using a UTF-16 string. Pass an empty String to free the previous value. This function is Windows-specific.

method.name: constant_win32_data_directory_type
method.description: Constant for the data directory type in sqlite3_win32_set_directory.

method.name: constant_win32_temp_directory_type
method.description: Constant for the temporary directory type in sqlite3_win32_set_directory.

method.name: get_autocommit
method.description: Returns true if the database connection is in autocommit mode, false otherwise. Autocommit mode is on by default and is disabled by BEGIN statements.

method.name: next_stmt
method.description: Returns the next prepared statement after the given statement. If stmt is null, returns the first prepared statement. Returns an empty Ref if no more statements are available.

method.name: rollback_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever a transaction is rolled back. Returns the previous callback's user data as uint64_t. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

method.name: release_memory
method.description: Attempts to free the specified number of bytes of heap memory by deallocating non-essential memory allocations such as database page caches. Returns the number of bytes actually freed, which might be more or less than the amount requested. Returns 0 if SQLite is not compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_MEMORY_MANAGEMENT.

method.name: hard_heap_limit
method.description: Sets or queries the hard upper bound on the amount of memory that will be allocated. If limit is negative, only the current limit is returned without making changes. Returns the previous limit size, or negative in case of error. Setting the limit to zero disables the heap limiter mechanism.

method.name: soft_heap_limit
method.description: Deprecated. Sets the soft limit on the amount of heap memory that may be allocated by SQLite. Use sqlite3_soft_heap_limit64() instead. Setting limit to 0 disables the heap limiter mechanism.

method.name: load_extension
method.description: Loads an SQLite extension library from the specified file. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or SQLITE_ERROR on failure. Extension loading must be enabled using sqlite3_enable_load_extension() or sqlite3_db_config() with SQLITE_DBCONFIG_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION before calling this function.

method.name: cancel_auto_extension
method.description: Unregisters an initialization routine that was registered using sqlite3_auto_extension. Returns 1 if the routine was successfully unregistered, 0 if it was not on the list. Note: This function uses a C language trick where the function pointer is declared with one signature but called with another. The full functionality is not easily translatable to higher-level languages. For now, this is a stub that returns 0.

method.name: reset_auto_extension
method.description: Disables all automatic extensions previously registered using sqlite3_auto_extension().

method.name: index_scan_unique
method.description: Virtual table scan flag: Scan visits at most 1 row.

method.name: index_scan_hex
method.description: Virtual table scan flag: Display idxNum as hex in EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN.

method.name: index_constraint_eq
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: = (equal).

method.name: index_constraint_gt
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: > (greater than).

method.name: index_constraint_le
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: <= (less than or equal).

method.name: index_constraint_lt
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: < (less than).

method.name: index_constraint_ge
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: >= (greater than or equal).

method.name: index_constraint_match
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: MATCH.

method.name: index_constraint_like
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: LIKE.

method.name: index_constraint_glob
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: GLOB.

method.name: index_constraint_regexp
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: REGEXP.

method.name: index_constraint_ne
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: != (not equal).

method.name: index_constraint_isnot
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: IS NOT.

method.name: index_constraint_isnotnull
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: IS NOT NULL.

method.name: index_constraint_isnull
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: IS NULL.

method.name: index_constraint_is
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: IS.

method.name: index_constraint_limit
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: LIMIT.

method.name: index_constraint_offset
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: OFFSET.

method.name: index_constraint_function
method.description: Virtual table constraint operator: Function (value 150, reserved for overloaded functions).

method.name: create_module
method.description: Registers a new virtual table module name on the database connection. The module name is registered with the provided methods and client data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

method.name: blob_open
method.description: Opens a BLOB handle for incremental I/O operations. The BLOB is located in row iRow, column zColumn, table zTable in database zDb. Parameter zDb is the symbolic name of the database (main/temp/attached). If flags is non-zero, the BLOB is opened for read/write access; if zero, read-only. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and stores the BLOB handle in out_blob. Otherwise returns an error code and sets out_blob handle to NULL.

method.name: vfs_register
method.description: Registers a Virtual File System (VFS). If make_default is true, this VFS becomes the default. The same VFS can be registered multiple times. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure.

method.name: vfs_unregister
method.description: Unregisters a Virtual File System (VFS). If the default VFS is unregistered, another VFS is chosen as default. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure.

method.name: mutex_free
method.description: Deallocates a previously allocated dynamic mutex. Attempting to deallocate a static mutex results in undefined behavior. After calling this function, the mutex handle is no longer valid.

method.name: mutex_enter
method.description: Enters a mutex. If another thread is already within the mutex, this function will block until the mutex is available. Mutexes created with SQLITE_MUTEX_RECURSIVE can be entered multiple times by the same thread.

method.name: mutex_try
method.description: Attempts to enter a mutex without blocking. Returns SQLITE_OK upon successful entry, or SQLITE_BUSY if another thread is already within the mutex. Mutexes created with SQLITE_MUTEX_RECURSIVE can be entered multiple times by the same thread. On systems that don't support this operation, this function will always return SQLITE_BUSY.

method.name: mutex_leave
method.description: Exits a mutex that was previously entered by the same thread. The behavior is undefined if the mutex is not currently entered by the calling thread or is not currently allocated.

method.name: db_mutex
method.description: Returns a pointer to the mutex that serializes access to the database connection when the threading mode is Serialized. Returns NULL if the threading mode is Single-thread or Multi-thread.

method.name: test_control_first
method.description: First valid operation code for sqlite3_test_control().

method.name: test_control_prng_save
method.description: Save the current state of the PRNG (Pseudo-Random Number Generator).

method.name: test_control_prng_restore
method.description: Restore the PRNG to its state at the time of the last SQLITE_TESTCTRL_PRNG_SAVE.

method.name: test_control_prng_reset
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_fk_no_action
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_bitvec_test
method.description: Test the bitvec functionality.

method.name: test_control_fault_install
method.description: Install fault injection routines.

method.name: test_control_benign_malloc_hooks
method.description: Install benign malloc hooks.

method.name: test_control_pending_byte
method.description: Set the pending byte for testing.

method.name: test_control_assert
method.description: Enable or disable assertions.

method.name: test_control_always
method.description: Always return the second parameter (used for testing).

method.name: test_control_reserve
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_json_selfcheck
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_optimizations
method.description: Get or set optimization flags.

method.name: test_control_iskeyword
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_getopt
method.description: Get option value.

method.name: test_control_scratchmalloc
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_internal_functions
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_localtime_fault
method.description: Inject a localtime fault.

method.name: test_control_explain_stmt
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_once_reset_threshold
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_never_corrupt
method.description: Set the 'never corrupt' flag.

method.name: test_control_vdbe_coverage
method.description: Enable or disable VDBE coverage counting.

method.name: test_control_byteorder
method.description: Get the byte order of the system.

method.name: test_control_isinit
method.description: Check if SQLite has been initialized.

method.name: test_control_sorter_mmap
method.description: Set the maximum number of memory-mapped files for sorter.

method.name: test_control_imposter
method.description: Enable or disable imposter tables.

method.name: test_control_parser_coverage
method.description: Enable or disable parser coverage counting.

method.name: test_control_result_intreal
method.description: Set the result type for integer-to-real conversions.

method.name: test_control_prng_seed
method.description: Set the seed for the PRNG.

method.name: test_control_extra_schema_checks
method.description: Enable extra schema checks.

method.name: test_control_seek_count
method.description: Get the seek count.

method.name: test_control_traceflags
method.description: Get or set trace flags.

method.name: test_control_tune
method.description: Get or set tuning parameters.

method.name: test_control_logest
method.description: Get or set logarithmic estimates.

method.name: test_control_uselongdouble
method.description: NOT USED. Legacy constant.

method.name: test_control_last
method.description: Largest TESTCTRL operation code.

method.name: keyword_count
method.description: Returns the number of distinct SQL keywords understood by SQLite.

method.name: keyword_name
method.description: Returns the keyword at the specified index (0-based). Returns an empty String if the index is out of bounds.

method.name: is_keyword
method.description: Checks if the given identifier is a SQL keyword. Returns true if it is a keyword, false otherwise.

method.name: str_new
method.description: Creates a new dynamic string object. The returned object must be freed by a subsequent call to str_finish(). If the db parameter is NULL, the maximum length of the string will be SQLITE_MAX_LENGTH; otherwise, it will be the value set for sqlite3_limit(D, SQLITE_LIMIT_LENGTH).

method.name: str_free
method.description: Destroys both the sqlite3_str object and the string content it contains. This is equivalent to calling sqlite3_free(sqlite3_str_finish(X)).

method.name: str_vappendf
method.description: Appends formatted text to the dynamic string using SQLite's printf functionality with a PackedStringArray of arguments.

method.name: str_append
method.description: Appends exactly N bytes from the text string onto the dynamic string. If length is negative, appends the entire string. If length is specified, appends at most that many bytes.

method.name: str_appendall
method.description: Appends the complete content of the zero-terminated string onto the dynamic string.

method.name: str_appendchar
method.description: Appends N copies of the single-byte character C onto the end of the dynamic string. The character is specified as an integer code point. Can be used for whitespace indentation.

method.name: str_reset
method.description: Resets the string under construction inside the sqlite3_str object back to zero bytes in length.

method.name: str_truncate
method.description: Changes the length of the string under construction to be N bytes or less. This routine is a no-op if N is negative or if the string is already N bytes or smaller in size.

method.name: str_length
method.description: Returns the current length in bytes of the dynamic string under construction. The returned length does not include the zero-termination byte.

method.name: str_value
method.description: Returns a pointer to the current content of the dynamic string under construction. The returned String is a copy and is safe to use after subsequent method calls on the same object.

method.name: status64
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information about SQLite's performance using 64-bit integers. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys as int64 values. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

method.name: status_memory_used
method.description: Current amount of memory checked out using sqlite3_malloc().

method.name: status_pagecache_used
method.description: Number of pages used out of the pagecache memory allocator.

method.name: status_pagecache_overflow
method.description: Number of bytes of page cache allocation which could not be satisfied by the pagecache buffer.

method.name: status_malloc_size
method.description: Largest memory allocation request handed to sqlite3_malloc() or sqlite3_realloc().

method.name: status_parser_stack
method.description: Deepest parser stack. Only meaningful if SQLite is compiled with YYTRACKMAXSTACKDEPTH.

method.name: status_pagecache_size
method.description: Largest memory allocation request handed to the pagecache memory allocator.

method.name: status_malloc_count
method.description: Number of separate memory allocations currently checked out.

method.name: db_status
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information about a single database connection. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

method.name: db_status64
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information about a single database connection using 64-bit integers. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys as int64 values. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

method.name: stmt_status
method.description: Retrieves and optionally resets counter values from a prepared statement. The op parameter specifies which counter to query, and reset_flag determines whether to reset the counter after reading. Returns the current value of the requested counter.

method.name: backup_step
method.description: Copies up to nPage pages between the source and destination databases.

method.name: backup_finish
method.description: Releases all resources associated with the backup operation and invalidates the backup object.

method.name: backup_remaining
method.description: Returns the number of pages still to be backed up at the conclusion of the most recent sqlite3_backup_step() call.

method.name: str_nicmp
method.description: Compares up to the first n characters of two UTF-8 strings in a case-independent fashion, using the same definition of case independence that SQLite uses internally for identifiers.

method.name: str_glob
method.description: Performs glob pattern matching where pattern is the first argument and string is the second. Returns zero if the string matches the glob pattern, non-zero otherwise. This function is case-sensitive and uses the same definition of glob pattern matching as SQLite's 'X GLOB P' operator.

method.name: str_like
method.description: Performs LIKE pattern matching where pattern is the first argument and string is the second. Returns zero if the string matches the LIKE pattern, non-zero otherwise. This function is case-insensitive (ASCII characters only) and uses the same definition of LIKE pattern matching as SQLite's 'X LIKE P ESCAPE E' operator. Set escape to 0 for patterns without an ESCAPE clause.

method.name: log
method.description: Writes a message to the SQLite error log. Takes an error code and a format string (printf-style). Note: Variadic arguments are not directly supported in this wrapper; prepare the formatted string in GDScript first using String::format() or similar before calling this function.

method.name: wal_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked each time data is committed to a database in WAL mode. The callback receives three arguments: the database handle, the database name ("main" or ATTACH-ed database name), and the number of pages in the WAL file. The callback should return an SQLite error code (typically SQLITE_OK for success). Returns the previous callback's user data as int64_t, or 0 if none was set.

method.name: vtab_config
method.description: Configures virtual table interface. This function may only be called from within xConnect or xCreate methods. Note: Variadic arguments are not supported in this wrapper; only the database handle and operation code are passed. For operations requiring additional parameters, operation-specific wrappers should be implemented.

method.name: vtab_on_conflict
method.description: This function may only be called from within a call to the xUpdate method of a virtual table implementation for an INSERT or UPDATE operation. The value returned is one of SQLITE_ROLLBACK, SQLITE_IGNORE, SQLITE_FAIL, SQLITE_ABORT, or SQLITE_REPLACE, according to the ON CONFLICT mode of the SQL statement that triggered the call to the xUpdate method of the virtual table.

method.name: vtab_in_next
method.description: These interfaces are only useful from within the xFilter() method of a virtual table implementation. The result of invoking these interfaces from any other context is undefined and probably harmful. Use these routines to access all values on the right-hand side of the IN constraint. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_value to point to the next value on the RHS of the IN constraint. If there are no more values, out_value is set to NULL and returns SQLITE_DONE. The return value might be some other value, such as SQLITE_NOMEM, in the event of a malfunction. The *ppOut values returned by these routines are only valid until the next call to either of these routines or until the end of the xFilter method from which these routines were called. If the virtual table implementation needs to retain the *ppOut values for longer, it must make copies.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus
method.description: This function returns information about the predicted and measured performance for pStmt. Advanced applications can use this interface to compare the predicted and the measured performance and issue warnings and/or rerun ANALYZE if discrepancies are found. Note: This is a simplified wrapper. The actual function writes to a void* pointer with type depending on scan_status_op. For production use, implement operation-specific wrappers with appropriate type handling.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_complex
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus_v2() to indicate that status information is available for all elements of a query plan that are reported by EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output, not just query loop elements.

method.name: snapshot_get
method.description: Attempts to make a new snapshot object capturing the current state of schema S in database connection D. Returns SQLITE_OK on success and writes the snapshot pointer to out_snapshot.

method.name: snapshot_free
method.description: Destroys a snapshot object to avoid memory leaks.

method.name: deserialize
method.description: Reopens a database from serialized data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: carray_bind_v2
method.description: Binds an array value to a parameter of a prepared statement for use with the carray() table-valued function. Uses SQLITE_STATIC (no copy, no destructor) internally. Advanced use cases requiring custom destructors should use the raw C API.

method.name: rtree_query_callback
method.description: Registers an R-tree query callback for spatial queries. The callback receives a query_info handle and must return an integer result code. Returns SQLITE_OK on success.

method.name: not_within
method.description: Object completely outside of query region.

method.name: partly_within
method.description: Object partially overlaps query region.

method.name: fully_within
method.description: Object fully contained within query region.

method.name: session_create
method.description: Create a new session object attached to database handle db. If successful, a pointer to the new object is written to out_session and SQLITE_OK is returned. Otherwise, out_session is not modified and an SQLite error code is returned.

method.name: session_enable
method.description: Enable or disable the recording of changes by a session object. Passing zero disables the session, greater than zero enables it, and less than zero is a no-op that queries the current state. Returns 1 if enabled, 0 if disabled.

method.name: session_memory_used
method.description: Return the total amount of heap memory in bytes currently used by the session object.

method.name: changeset_start_v2
method.description: Create an iterator to traverse a changeset with additional flags. On success, out_iter contains the iterator handle. The changeset buffer must remain valid until the iterator is destroyed.

method.name: changeset_next
method.description: Advance a changeset iterator to the next change. Returns SQLITE_ROW if a change is available, SQLITE_DONE if no more changes, or an error code.

method.name: changeset_concat
method.description: Concatenate two changesets, A and B, into a single changeset equivalent to applying A followed by B. Returns an Array with [error_code, concatenated_changeset]. The caller is responsible for memory management of the output buffer via SQLite's sqlite3_free().

method.name: changegroup_new
method.description: Create a new changegroup object. A changegroup is used to combine two or more changesets or patchsets into a single changeset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success and sets out_handle to the new changegroup handle. The caller must eventually free the handle using sqlite3changegroup_delete().

method.name: rebaser_create
method.description: Creates a new sqlite3_rebaser object. Returns a Ref to the handle, or an empty Ref on error.

method.name: rebaser_configure
method.description: Configures the rebaser with rebase data from sqlite3changeset_apply_v2(). Can be called multiple times to configure with multiple rebase buffers.

method.name: rebaser_destroy
method.description: Destroys the sqlite3_rebaser object and frees associated resources.

method.name: changeset_concat_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_concat. Concatenates two changesets into one. Returns the concatenated changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: changeset_start_v2_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_start_v2. Starts iterating over a changeset with configuration flags. Returns a Ref to the iterator handle, or an empty Ref on error.

method.name: session_changeset_stream
method.description: Streaming version of session_changeset. Extracts a changeset from a session. Returns the changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: session_patchset_stream
method.description: Streaming version of session_patchset. Extracts a patchset from a session. Returns the patchset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: changegroup_add_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changegroup_add. Adds changes from a PackedByteArray to a changegroup. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure.

method.name: session_config_strmsize
method.description: Operation code for sqlite3session_config() to configure streaming data chunk size.

method.name: close_v2
method.description: Closes a database connection. Returns SQLITE_OK regardless of the state, but marks the connection as a zombie and deallocates it later when all prepared statements are finalized, all BLOB handles are closed, and all backups have finished.

method.name: exec
method.description: Executes zero or more semicolon-separated SQL statements. If callback is provided, it is invoked for each result row with (PackedStringArray values, PackedStringArray column_names). Returning non-zero from the callback stops execution. Use get_last_error() to retrieve the error message if the return value is not SQLITE_OK.

method.name: last_insert_rowid
method.description: Returns the rowid of the most recent successful INSERT into a rowid table. Returns zero if no successful INSERTs have occurred.

method.name: set_last_insert_rowid
method.description: Allows the application to set the value returned by calling sqlite3_last_insert_rowid(D) to R without inserting a row into the database.

method.name: changes
method.description: Returns the number of rows modified, inserted or deleted by the most recently completed INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE statement on the database connection.

method.name: total_changes
method.description: Returns the total number of rows inserted, modified or deleted by all INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE statements completed since the database connection was opened, including those executed as part of trigger programs.

method.name: complete
method.description: Determines if an SQL statement is complete. Returns 1 if the input string appears to be a complete SQL statement, 0 if incomplete, or SQLITE_NOMEM if a memory allocation fails.

method.name: busy_timeout
method.description: Sets a busy handler that sleeps for a specified amount of time when a table is locked. Calling with ms ≤ 0 turns off all busy handlers.

method.name: setlk_timeout
method.description: Sets the setlk timeout for blocking locks on WAL-mode databases. Only available in SQLITE_ENABLE_SETLK_TIMEOUT builds. Passing 0 disables blocking locks, -1 requests indefinite blocking.

method.name: setlk_block_on_connect_flag
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_setlk_timeout(): block on connect when the number of connections falls from 1 to 0.

method.name: get_table
method.description: Executes one or more semicolon-separated SQL statements and returns the result as a 2D array. The first row contains column names. This is a legacy interface; use of this interface is not recommended.

method.name: deny_code
method.description: Authorizer return code to abort the SQL statement with an error.

method.name: ignore_code
method.description: Authorizer return code to deny access but not generate an error.

method.name: create_index_action
method.description: Action code for creating an index.

method.name: create_table_action
method.description: Action code for creating a table.

method.name: create_temp_index_action
method.description: Action code for creating a temporary index.

method.name: create_temp_table_action
method.description: Action code for creating a temporary table.

method.name: create_temp_trigger_action
method.description: Action code for creating a temporary trigger.

method.name: create_temp_view_action
method.description: Action code for creating a temporary view.

method.name: create_trigger_action
method.description: Action code for creating a trigger.

method.name: create_view_action
method.description: Action code for creating a view.

method.name: delete_action
method.description: Action code for deleting rows.

method.name: drop_index_action
method.description: Action code for dropping an index.

method.name: drop_table_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a table.

method.name: drop_temp_index_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a temporary index.

method.name: drop_temp_table_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a temporary table.

method.name: drop_temp_trigger_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a temporary trigger.

method.name: drop_temp_view_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a temporary view.

method.name: drop_trigger_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a trigger.

method.name: drop_view_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a view.

method.name: insert_action
method.description: Action code for inserting rows.

method.name: pragma_action
method.description: Action code for pragma operations.

method.name: read_action
method.description: Action code for reading table columns.

method.name: select_action
method.description: Action code for SELECT statements.

method.name: transaction_action
method.description: Action code for transaction operations.

method.name: update_action
method.description: Action code for updating rows.

method.name: attach_action
method.description: Action code for ATTACH DATABASE.

method.name: detach_action
method.description: Action code for DETACH DATABASE.

method.name: alter_table_action
method.description: Action code for ALTER TABLE.

method.name: reindex_action
method.description: Action code for REINDEX.

method.name: analyze_action
method.description: Action code for ANALYZE.

method.name: create_vtable_action
method.description: Action code for creating a virtual table.

method.name: drop_vtable_action
method.description: Action code for dropping a virtual table.

method.name: function_action
method.description: Action code for SQL function calls.

method.name: savepoint_action
method.description: Action code for savepoint operations.

method.name: copy_action
method.description: Action code no longer used.

method.name: recursive_action
method.description: Action code for recursive CTEs.

method.name: profile
method.description: DEPRECATED: Registers a profile callback for SQL statement execution timing. The callback receives the SQL statement text as a String and the elapsed time in nanoseconds (with millisecond resolution in current implementation). Use sqlite3_trace_v2() instead for new code.

method.name: trace_stmt_mask
method.description: Trace event code for statement execution (SQL text or trigger invocation).

method.name: trace_profile_mask
method.description: Trace event code for statement profiling (execution time).

method.name: trace_row_mask
method.description: Trace event code for row results (each row of output).

method.name: trace_close_mask
method.description: Trace event code for database connection closure.

method.name: progress_handler
method.description: Sets a progress handler callback that is invoked periodically during long-running operations. The callback receives no arguments. If it returns non-zero, the operation is interrupted. The `n` parameter specifies the number of virtual machine instructions between callbacks; if less than 1, the handler is disabled.

method.name: open
method.description: Opens an SQLite database connection. The `filename` can be a file path, ":memory:" for in-memory database, or empty string for temporary on-disk database. On success, `out_db` contains a valid handle and returns SQLITE_OK; otherwise, returns an error code and `out_db` remains invalid.

method.name: open16
method.description: Opens an SQLite database connection using a UTF-16 encoded filename. Use `open()` for UTF-8 filenames unless UTF-16 is specifically required.

method.name: open_v2
method.description: Opens an SQLite database connection with additional control via flags and VFS. The `flags` parameter specifies access mode and options (e.g., SQLITE_OPEN_READONLY). The `vfs` parameter specifies the VFS module name (empty string uses default).

method.name: errmsg16
method.description: Returns the English-language error message (UTF-16 in native byte order) for the most recent error on the database connection. Returns an empty String if no error message is available. The message is managed internally and may be overwritten by subsequent API calls.

method.name: error_offset
method.description: Returns the byte offset (in UTF-8) of the start of the token that caused the most recent error. Returns -1 if the error does not reference a specific token (e.g., memory allocation failure).

method.name: set_errmsg
method.description: Sets the error code and message for the database handle. If errmsg is empty, the default message for errcode is used. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_NOMEM on OOM, or SQLITE_MISUSE for invalid handle.

method.name: limit_length
method.description: Maximum size of any string or BLOB or table row, in bytes.

method.name: limit_sql_length
method.description: Maximum length of an SQL statement, in bytes.

method.name: limit_column
method.description: Maximum number of columns in a table definition or in the result set of a SELECT or in an index or in an ORDER BY or GROUP BY clause.

method.name: limit_expr_depth
method.description: Maximum depth of the parse tree on any expression.

method.name: limit_compound_select
method.description: Maximum number of terms in a compound SELECT statement.

method.name: limit_vdbe_op
method.description: Maximum number of instructions in a virtual machine program used to implement an SQL statement.

method.name: limit_function_arg
method.description: Maximum number of arguments on a function.

method.name: limit_attached
method.description: Maximum number of attached databases.

method.name: limit_like_pattern_length
method.description: Maximum length of the pattern argument to the LIKE or GLOB operators.

method.name: limit_variable_number
method.description: Maximum index number of any parameter in an SQL statement.

method.name: limit_trigger_depth
method.description: Maximum depth of recursion for triggers.

method.name: limit_worker_threads
method.description: Maximum number of auxiliary worker threads that a single prepared statement may start.

method.name: limit_parser_depth
method.description: Maximum depth of the LALR(1) parser stack used to analyze input SQL statements.

method.name: prepare_persistent_flag
method.description: Hint to the query planner that the prepared statement will be retained for a long time and probably reused many times.

method.name: prepare_normalize_flag
method.description: No-op flag. Previously required for using sqlite3_normalized_sql(), but now available to all prepared statements.

method.name: prepare_no_vtab_flag
method.description: Causes the SQL compiler to return an error if the statement uses any virtual tables.

method.name: prepare_dont_log_flag
method.description: Prevents SQL compiler errors from being sent to the error log. Useful for test compiles without generating error messages.

method.name: prepare_from_ddl_flag
method.description: Causes the SQL compiler to enforce security constraints as if the SQL statement came from an attacker. Use when preparing SQL derived from database schema.

method.name: prepare
method.description: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement. Use sqlite3_prepare_v2() for new code as it's recommended over this legacy interface. Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string.

method.name: prepare_v2
method.description: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using the recommended sqlite3_prepare_v2() interface. Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. This version automatically recompiles on schema changes and returns detailed error codes.

method.name: prepare_v3
method.description: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare_v3() with custom prepFlags. Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string.

method.name: prepare16
method.description: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare16() (UTF-16 version). Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. Note: The UTF-8 versions (prepare, prepare_v2, prepare_v3) are recommended for new code.

method.name: prepare16_v2
method.description: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare16_v2() (UTF-16 version). Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. This version automatically recompiles on schema changes and returns detailed error codes. Note: The UTF-8 versions (prepare, prepare_v2, prepare_v3) are recommended for new code.

method.name: prepare16_v3
method.description: Compiles the first SQL statement from the input string into a prepared statement using sqlite3_prepare16_v3() (UTF-16 version with custom prepFlags). Returns SQLITE_OK on success with out_stmt containing the prepared statement handle. out_tail receives the unused portion of the SQL string. Note: The UTF-8 versions (prepare, prepare_v2, prepare_v3) are recommended for new code.

method.name: get_sql
method.description: Returns the UTF-8 SQL text used to create the prepared statement. The returned string is managed by SQLite and is automatically freed when the prepared statement is finalized.

method.name: get_normalized_sql
method.description: Returns the UTF-8 normalized SQL text of the prepared statement. The semantics used to normalize a SQL statement are unspecified and subject to change. At a minimum, literal values will be replaced with suitable placeholders. This function is only available if SQLITE_ENABLE_NORMALIZE is defined.

method.name: is_read_only
method.description: Returns true if the prepared statement makes no direct changes to the database file. Note that application-defined SQL functions or virtual tables might change the database indirectly as a side effect.

method.name: is_explain
method.description: Returns 1 if the prepared statement is an EXPLAIN statement, 2 if it is an EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN statement, or 0 if it is an ordinary statement or NULL.

method.name: set_explain_mode
method.description: Changes the EXPLAIN setting for the prepared statement. If mode is 0, the statement becomes normal. If mode is 1, it behaves as if its SQL text began with 'EXPLAIN'. If mode is 2, it behaves as if its SQL text began with 'EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN'. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: is_busy
method.description: Returns true if the prepared statement has been stepped at least once using sqlite3_step() but has neither run to completion (returned SQLITE_DONE) nor been reset using sqlite3_reset(). Returns false if the statement is not busy or is NULL.

method.name: bind_double
method.description: Binds a double-precision floating-point value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: bind_int
method.description: Binds a 32-bit integer value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: bind_null
method.description: Binds a NULL value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: bind_text
method.description: Binds a UTF-8 text string to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If text is empty, binds NULL instead. The string is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

method.name: bind_text16
method.description: Binds a UTF-16 text string to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If text is empty, binds NULL instead. The string is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

method.name: bind_text64
method.description: Binds a UTF-8 text string to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. If text is empty, binds NULL instead. The string is copied and managed by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT).

method.name: bind_value
method.description: Binds a value from an sqlite3_value object to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: bind_pointer
method.description: Binds a pointer value to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise. The pointer is stored as an int64_t for compatibility with Godot's binding system.

method.name: bind_zeroblob64
method.description: Binds a zero-filled BLOB of specified 64-bit length to a parameter in the prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: bind_parameter_count
method.description: Returns the number of SQL parameters in the prepared statement. This is the index of the largest (rightmost) parameter, which corresponds to the number of unique parameters for most parameter forms.

method.name: bind_parameter_name
method.description: Returns the name of the N-th SQL parameter in the prepared statement. Returns an empty String if the parameter has no name or if the index is out of range.

method.name: bind_parameter_index
method.description: Returns the index of an SQL parameter given its name. Returns 0 if no matching parameter is found.

method.name: clear_bindings
method.description: Resets all host parameters on the prepared statement to NULL. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, error code otherwise.

method.name: column_count
method.description: Returns the number of columns in the result set returned by the prepared statement. Returns 0 if the statement returns no data (e.g., an UPDATE statement).

method.name: column_name
method.description: Returns the name assigned to a particular column in the result set. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_database_name
method.description: Returns the database name that is the origin of a particular result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_table_name16
method.description: Returns the table name that is the origin of a particular result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.
