method.name: column_origin_name
method.description: Returns the column name that is the origin of a particular result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_origin_name16
method.description: Returns the column name that is the origin of a particular result column as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the name is NULL.

method.name: column_decltype
method.description: Returns the declared datatype of a query result column. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the column is an expression or subquery.

method.name: step
method.description: Evaluates a prepared statement. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise. For SELECT statements, SQLITE_ROW is returned when a new row of data is ready, and SQLITE_DONE is returned when the statement has finished executing.

method.name: data_count
method.description: Returns the number of columns in the current row of the result set. Returns 0 if the prepared statement does not have results ready or if the previous call to step() returned SQLITE_DONE.

method.name: constant_integer
method.description: 64-bit signed integer type code.

method.name: constant_float
method.description: 64-bit IEEE floating point number type code.

method.name: constant_blob
method.description: BLOB type code.

method.name: constant_null
method.description: NULL type code.

method.name: column_text
method.description: Returns a UTF-8 TEXT result from a query as a String. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_text16
method.description: Returns a UTF-16 TEXT result from a query as a String. Returns an empty String if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_value
method.description: Returns the result column as an sqlite3_value object. Note: The returned value is an unprotected sqlite3_value object and should only be used safely with bind_value() and result_value() in multithreaded environments. Returns an empty Ref if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_bytes
method.description: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-8 TEXT result. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_bytes16
method.description: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-16 TEXT result. Returns 0 if the column index is out of range or if the result is NULL.

method.name: column_type
method.description: Returns the default datatype code of the result column (SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_FLOAT, SQLITE_TEXT, SQLITE_BLOB, or SQLITE_NULL). Returns SQLITE_NULL if the column index is out of range.

method.name: finalize
method.description: Destroys a prepared statement object. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful, or an error code if the most recent evaluation encountered errors. Calling with a null statement is a no-op.

method.name: reset
method.description: Resets a prepared statement object back to its initial state, ready to be re-executed. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful, or an error code if the most recent evaluation encountered errors. Bound variables retain their values.

method.name: create_function
method.description: Creates or redefines a SQL function. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly. For full functionality, proper handling of sqlite3_context and sqlite3_value is required.

method.name: create_function16
method.description: Creates or redefines a SQL function with UTF-16 function name. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

method.name: create_function_v2
method.description: Creates or redefines a SQL function with destructor callback. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

method.name: create_window_function
method.description: Creates or redefines a SQL window function. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

method.name: constant_utf8
method.description: Text is encoded as UTF-8.

method.name: constant_utf16le
method.description: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with little endian byte order.

method.name: constant_utf16be
method.description: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with big endian byte order.

method.name: constant_utf16
method.description: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with native byte order.

method.name: constant_any
method.description: Deprecated encoding value. Do not use in new applications.

method.name: constant_utf16_aligned
method.description: Text is encoded as UTF-16 with native byte order and 2-byte alignment.

method.name: constant_utf8_zt
method.description: Zero-terminated UTF-8 encoding.

method.name: constant_deterministic
method.description: Function is deterministic (same output for same inputs).

method.name: constant_directonly
method.description: Function may only be invoked from top-level SQL.

method.name: constant_subtype
method.description: Function may inspect argument subtypes.

method.name: constant_innocuous
method.description: Function is unlikely to cause problems even if misused.

method.name: constant_result_subtype
method.description: Function may set result subtype.

method.name: constant_selforder1
method.description: Function is an aggregate that internally orders values.

method.name: aggregate_count
method.description: Deprecated: Returns the number of values passed to the aggregate function.

method.name: expired
method.description: Deprecated: Returns non-zero if the prepared statement has been invalidated.

method.name: transfer_bindings
method.description: Deprecated: Transfers bindings from one prepared statement to another.

method.name: global_recover
method.description: Deprecated: Recovers from a memory allocation failure.

method.name: thread_cleanup
method.description: Deprecated: Cleans up thread-specific data.

method.name: memory_alarm
method.description: Deprecated: Sets a memory alarm callback. Note: This is a simplified wrapper that may not handle all callback scenarios properly.

method.name: value_text
method.description: Extracts a UTF-8 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

method.name: value_text16
method.description: Extracts a UTF-16 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

method.name: value_text16le
method.description: Extracts a UTF-16 little-endian TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

method.name: value_text16be
method.description: Extracts a UTF-16 big-endian TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object as a String. Returns an empty String if the value handle is invalid or if the value is NULL.

method.name: value_bytes16
method.description: Returns the size in bytes of a BLOB or UTF-16 TEXT value from a protected sqlite3_value object. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_type
method.description: Returns the default datatype code of the value from a protected sqlite3_value object (SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_FLOAT, SQLITE_TEXT, SQLITE_BLOB, or SQLITE_NULL). Returns SQLITE_NULL if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_numeric_type
method.description: Returns the numeric datatype code of the value from a protected sqlite3_value object (SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_FLOAT, or SQLITE_NULL). Returns SQLITE_NULL if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_nochange
method.description: Returns 1 if the value is part of a row that is being updated in a UPDATE statement and the value has not changed, and 0 otherwise. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_frombind
method.description: Returns 1 if the value originated from a bound parameter in an SQL statement, and 0 otherwise. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_encoding
method.description: Returns the internal text encoding state of an sqlite3_value object (SQLITE_UTF8, SQLITE_UTF16BE, or SQLITE_UTF16LE) if the value is of TEXT type. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_subtype
method.description: Returns the subtype for an application-defined SQL function argument. Returns 0 if the value handle is invalid.

method.name: value_dup
method.description: Makes a copy of the sqlite3_value object V and returns a pointer to that copy. Returns NULL if V is NULL or if a memory allocation fails. If V is a pointer value, then the result is a NULL value.

method.name: aggregate_context
method.description: Allocates memory for storing aggregate function state. On first call, allocates N bytes, zeroes them, and returns pointer. On subsequent calls for the same aggregate function instance, returns the same buffer. Returns 0 if context is invalid or nBytes <= 0.

method.name: user_data
method.description: Returns the user data pointer that was passed when the function was registered. Returns 0 if the context is invalid.

method.name: context_db_handle
method.description: Returns the database connection handle associated with the function context. Returns NULL if the context is invalid.

method.name: get_auxdata
method.description: Returns the auxiliary data associated with the Nth argument of the application-defined function. Returns 0 if there is no auxiliary data or if the context is invalid.

method.name: get_clientdata
method.description: Returns the client data pointer associated with the database connection and name. Returns 0 if there is no associated data or if the database handle is invalid.

method.name: result_error
method.description: Causes the implemented SQL function to throw an error with the specified message. Uses -1 for the length parameter to let SQLite search for the null terminator.

method.name: result_error_toobig
method.description: Causes SQLite to throw an error indicating that a string or BLOB is too long to represent.

method.name: result_error_code
method.description: Changes the error code returned by SQLite as a result of an error in a function.

method.name: result_int
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the 32-bit signed integer value specified.

method.name: result_int64
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the 64-bit signed integer value specified.

method.name: result_null
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be NULL.

method.name: result_text
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-8 text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

method.name: result_text64
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the text string specified in the given encoding. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

method.name: result_text16
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-16 text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

method.name: result_text16le
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-16 little endian text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied).

method.name: result_text16be
method.description: Sets the return value of an application-defined function to be the UTF-16 big endian text string specified. Uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT as the destructor (data is copied). Note: The input string should be in big endian format if the system's native byte order is not big endian.

method.name: result_pointer
method.description: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a pointer. The destructor parameter is omitted (uses nullptr).

method.name: result_zeroblob
method.description: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a BLOB of zeros containing n bytes.

method.name: result_zeroblob64
method.description: Sets the result of an application-defined function to be a BLOB of zeros containing n bytes. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code on failure.

method.name: result_subtype
method.description: Sets the subtype of the result from an application-defined SQL function. Only the lower 8 bits of the subtype are preserved in current versions of SQLite. The function should be used with the SQLITE_RESULT_SUBTYPE property when registering the SQL function.

method.name: create_collation
method.description: Adds a new collation to the database connection. The compare_func callback receives two String arguments and must return an integer that is negative, zero, or positive if the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second, respectively.

method.name: create_collation16
method.description: Adds a new collation to the database connection with a UTF-16 name. The compare_func callback receives two String arguments and must return an integer that is negative, zero, or positive if the first string is less than, equal to, or greater than the second, respectively.

method.name: collation_needed
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever an undefined collation sequence is required. The callback receives three arguments: a Ref<Sqlite3Handle> for the database connection, an integer for the text representation, and a String for the collation name.

method.name: activate_cerod
method.description: Activates the CEROD (Cryptography Enabled Read-Only Database) functionality with the specified passphrase. This function is only available when SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_CEROD.

method.name: sleep
method.description: Suspends execution for at least the specified number of milliseconds. Returns the actual number of milliseconds slept. If a negative argument is passed, it is changed to zero.

method.name: db_handle
method.description: Returns the database connection handle to which the prepared statement belongs.

method.name: db_name
method.description: Returns the schema name for the N-th database on the database connection. Returns an empty String if N is out of range. Note: The returned string is managed by SQLite and may be deallocated by schema changes.

method.name: db_filename
method.description: Returns the absolute pathname of the filename associated with the specified database. Returns an empty String if the database doesn't exist or is temporary/in-memory.

method.name: db_readonly
method.description: Returns 1 if the database is read-only, 0 if it is read/write, or -1 if the database name is not valid.

method.name: txn_state
method.description: Returns the transaction state of the schema: 0 (SQLITE_TXN_NONE) for no transaction, 1 (SQLITE_TXN_READ) for read transaction, 2 (SQLITE_TXN_WRITE) for write transaction, or -1 if the schema name is not valid.

method.name: constant_txn_none
method.description: Constant indicating no transaction is currently pending.

method.name: constant_txn_read
method.description: Constant indicating the database is currently in a read transaction.

method.name: constant_txn_write
method.description: Constant indicating the database is currently in a write transaction.

method.name: commit_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever a transaction is committed. If the callback returns non-zero, the commit is converted to a rollback. Returns the previous callback's user data as uint64_t. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

method.name: autovacuum_pages
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked prior to each autovacuum of the database file. The callback receives the schema name, database file size in pages, number of free pages, and bytes per page as arguments. It should return the number of free pages to remove. Returns SQLITE_OK (0) on success or an error code on failure. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

method.name: update_hook
method.description: Registers a callback to be invoked when a row is updated, inserted, or deleted in a rowid table. The callback receives the operation type (SQLITE_INSERT, SQLITE_DELETE, or SQLITE_UPDATE), database name, table name, and rowid as arguments. Returns the previous callback's user data as uint64_t. Registering an invalid Callable disables the callback.

method.name: enable_shared_cache
method.description: Enables or disables the sharing of the database cache and schema data structures between connections to the same database. This is a global setting that affects all subsequent calls to sqlite3_open(), sqlite3_open_v2(), and sqlite3_open16(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

method.name: table_column_metadata
method.description: Extracts metadata about a column of a table. Returns a Dictionary with keys: data_type, collation_sequence, not_null, primary_key, and auto_increment. Returns an empty Dictionary if the column does not exist or an error occurs.

method.name: enable_load_extension
method.description: Enables or disables extension loading for the specified database connection. This enables or disables both the C-API sqlite3_load_extension() and the SQL function load_extension(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise. For security reasons, consider using sqlite3_db_config() with SQLITE_DBCONFIG_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION to enable only the C-API.

method.name: auto_extension
method.description: Registers an entry point to be automatically invoked for each new database connection. Note: This function uses a C language trick where the function pointer is declared with one signature but called with another. The full functionality is not easily translatable to higher-level languages. For now, this is a stub that returns SQLITE_OK.

method.name: overload_function
method.description: Ensures a global version of a function exists so it can be overloaded by a virtual table. If the function doesn't exist, it's created with an implementation that throws an exception. The nArg parameter specifies the number of arguments (-1 for any number).

method.name: blob_reopen
method.description: Moves an existing BLOB handle to a new row of the same database table. The new row is identified by the rowid value. Only the row can be changed - the database, table and column remain the same. This is faster than closing the existing handle and opening a new one. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code if the new row doesn't exist or doesn't contain a blob/text value.

method.name: blob_close
method.description: Closes an open BLOB handle. The handle is closed unconditionally even if this routine returns an error code. If the blob handle was opened for read-write access and the database is in auto-commit mode with no other active write operations, the current transaction is committed. Passing NULL or an invalid handle is a harmless no-op.

method.name: blob_bytes
method.description: Returns the size in bytes of the BLOB accessible via the BLOB handle. This routine only works on successfully opened BLOB handles that have not been closed. Returns -1 if the handle is invalid.

method.name: blob_read
method.description: Reads data from an open BLOB handle into a PackedByteArray. Reads 'size' bytes starting from 'offset' in the BLOB. Returns an empty PackedByteArray on error. The offset and size must be valid (offset >= 0, size >= 0, offset + size <= blob size).

method.name: blob_write
method.description: Writes data into an open BLOB handle. Writes the contents of 'data' starting at 'offset' in the BLOB. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_READONLY if the BLOB handle was not opened for writing, or other error codes for various failure conditions. Cannot increase the size of the BLOB.

method.name: vfs_find
method.description: Finds a Virtual File System (VFS) by name. Returns a handle to the VFS if found, or an empty handle if not found. Names are case-sensitive. If vfs_name is empty, returns the default VFS.

method.name: mutex_held
method.description: Returns true if the mutex is held by the calling thread. This routine is intended for use inside assert() statements. If the mutex handle is NULL, returns true (to avoid spurious assertion failures when mutexes are disabled).

method.name: mutex_notheld
method.description: Returns true if the mutex is NOT held by the calling thread. This routine is intended for use inside assert() statements. If the mutex handle is NULL, returns true (to avoid spurious assertion failures when mutexes are disabled).

method.name: mutex_fast
method.description: Mutex type for fast (non-reentrant) mutex allocation.

method.name: mutex_recursive
method.description: Mutex type for recursive mutex allocation.

method.name: mutex_static_main
method.description: Mutex type for the main mutex (sqlite3_mutex_alloc() for static mutexes).

method.name: mutex_static_mem
method.description: Mutex type for memory allocation mutex (sqlite3_malloc()).

method.name: mutex_static_mem2
method.description: Mutex type for NOT USED mutex (legacy).

method.name: mutex_static_open
method.description: Mutex type for sqlite3BtreeOpen() mutex.

method.name: mutex_static_prng
method.description: Mutex type for sqlite3_randomness() mutex.

method.name: mutex_static_lru
method.description: Mutex type for LRU page list mutex.

method.name: mutex_static_lru2
method.description: Mutex type for NOT USED mutex (legacy).

method.name: mutex_static_pmem
method.description: Mutex type for sqlite3PageMalloc() mutex.

method.name: mutex_static_app1
method.description: Mutex type for application use 1.

method.name: mutex_static_app2
method.description: Mutex type for application use 2.

method.name: mutex_static_app3
method.description: Mutex type for application use 3.

method.name: mutex_static_vfs1
method.description: Mutex type for built-in VFS use.

method.name: mutex_static_vfs2
method.description: Mutex type for extension VFS use.

method.name: mutex_static_vfs3
method.description: Mutex type for application VFS use.

method.name: mutex_static_master
method.description: Legacy alias for SQLITE_MUTEX_STATIC_MAIN (same value).

method.name: file_control
method.description: Performs a file control operation on a database file. The db_name parameter specifies the database ("main", "temp", or an attached database name); NULL or empty string means "main". The op parameter is the file control opcode, and arg is a pointer-sized value passed to the xFileControl method. Returns the result of the xFileControl method or SQLITE_ERROR if the database name is invalid.

method.name: test_control
method.description: Testing interface for SQLite. This function is for testing/internal use only and may change without notice between releases. It is not guaranteed to exist in all builds. Note: This is a simplified wrapper for the variadic function; full control requires using the raw C API directly.

method.name: str_finish
method.description: Destroys the sqlite3_str object and returns the constructed string. The returned string is automatically freed after conversion to a Godot String. Returns an empty String if the input is invalid or if the string is zero bytes long.

method.name: db_status_lookaside_used
method.description: Returns the number of lookaside memory slots currently checked out.

method.name: db_status_cache_used
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by all pager caches associated with the database connection. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_schema_used
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the schema for all databases associated with the connection. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_stmt_used
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap and lookaside memory used by all prepared statements associated with the database connection. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_lookaside_hit
method.description: Returns the number of malloc attempts that were satisfied using lookaside memory. Only the high-water value is meaningful; the current value is always zero.

method.name: db_status_lookaside_miss_size
method.description: Returns the number of malloc attempts that might have been satisfied using lookaside memory but failed due to the amount of memory requested being larger than the lookaside slot size. Only the high-water value is meaningful; the current value is always zero.

method.name: db_status_lookaside_miss_full
method.description: Returns the number of malloc attempts that might have been satisfied using lookaside memory but failed due to all lookaside memory already being in use. Only the high-water value is meaningful; the current value is always zero.

method.name: db_status_cache_hit
method.description: Returns the number of pager cache hits that have occurred. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_cache_miss
method.description: Returns the number of pager cache misses that have occurred. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_cache_write
method.description: Returns the number of dirty cache entries that have been written to disk. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_deferred_fks
method.description: Returns zero for the current value if and only if all foreign key constraints have been resolved. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_cache_used_shared
method.description: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by all pager caches, divided evenly among shared connections. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_cache_spill
method.description: Returns the number of dirty cache entries that have been written to disk in the middle of a transaction. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: db_status_tempbuf_spill
method.description: Returns the number of bytes written to temporary files on disk that could have been kept in memory. The highwater mark is always 0.

method.name: stmtstatus_fullscan_step
method.description: This is the number of times that SQLite has stepped forward in a table as part of a full table scan. Large numbers for this counter may indicate opportunities for performance improvement through careful use of indices.

method.name: stmtstatus_sort
method.description: This is the number of sort operations that have occurred. A non-zero value in this counter may indicate an opportunity to improve performance through careful use of indices.

method.name: stmtstatus_autoindex
method.description: This is the number of rows inserted into transient indices that were created automatically in order to help joins run faster. A non-zero value in this counter may indicate an opportunity to improve performance by adding permanent indices that do not need to be reinitialized each time the statement is run.

method.name: stmtstatus_vm_step
method.description: This is the number of virtual machine operations executed by the prepared statement if that number is less than or equal to 2147483647. The number of virtual machine operations can be used as a proxy for the total work done by the prepared statement. If the number of virtual machine operations exceeds 2147483647 then the value returned by this statement status code is undefined.

method.name: stmtstatus_reprepare
method.description: This is the number of times that the prepare statement has been automatically regenerated due to schema changes or changes to bound parameters that might affect the query plan.

method.name: stmtstatus_run
method.description: This is the number of times that the prepared statement has been run. A single "run" for the purposes of this counter is one or more calls to sqlite3_step() followed by a call to sqlite3_reset(). The counter is incremented on the first sqlite3_step() call of each cycle.

method.name: stmtstatus_filter_miss
method.description: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_HIT is the number of times that a join step was bypassed because a Bloom filter returned not-found. The corresponding SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_MISS value is the number of times that the Bloom filter returned a find, and thus the join step had to be processed as normal.

method.name: stmtstatus_filter_hit
method.description: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_HIT is the number of times that a join step was bypassed because a Bloom filter returned not-found. The corresponding SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_MISS value is the number of times that the Bloom filter returned a find, and thus the join step had to be processed as normal.

method.name: stmtstatus_memused
method.description: This is the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the prepared statement. This value is not actually a counter, and so the resetFlg parameter to sqlite3_stmt_status() is ignored when the opcode is SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_MEMUSED.

method.name: backup_pagecount
method.description: Returns the total number of pages in the source database at the conclusion of the most recent sqlite3_backup_step() call.

method.name: str_icmp
method.description: Compares two UTF-8 strings in a case-independent fashion, using the same definition of case independence that SQLite uses internally for identifiers.

method.name: wal_autocheckpoint
method.description: Configures automatic checkpointing for a database connection in WAL mode. The database will automatically checkpoint after committing a transaction if there are 'threshold' or more frames in the write-ahead log file. Pass zero or a negative value to disable automatic checkpoints. This replaces any existing callback registered using sqlite3_wal_hook(). Returns an SQLite error code (SQLITE_OK on success).

method.name: wal_checkpoint
method.description: Performs a checkpoint on a database in WAL mode. This transfers content from the write-ahead log (WAL) into the database file and resets the WAL. Equivalent to sqlite3_wal_checkpoint_v2() with passive mode. Returns an SQLite error code (SQLITE_OK on success).

method.name: wal_checkpoint_v2
method.description: Runs a checkpoint operation on a database in WAL mode with specified mode. Returns an Array with [error_code, wal_log_size, total_ckpt_frames]. The mode parameter should be one of SQLITE_CHECKPOINT_PASSIVE, FULL, RESTART, TRUNCATE, or NOOP. For database_name, pass empty string or null to checkpoint all attached databases.

method.name: checkpoint_noop
method.description: Do no work at all

method.name: checkpoint_passive
method.description: Do as much as possible without blocking

method.name: checkpoint_full
method.description: Wait for writers, then checkpoint

method.name: checkpoint_restart
method.description: Like FULL but wait for readers

method.name: checkpoint_truncate
method.description: Like RESTART but also truncate WAL

method.name: vtab_constraint_support
method.description: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_CONSTRAINT_SUPPORT,X) are supported, where X is an integer. If X is zero, then the virtual table does not support constraints. In this configuration (which is the default) if a call to the xUpdate method returns SQLITE_CONSTRAINT, then the entire statement is rolled back as if ON CONFLICT OR ABORT had been specified as part of the user's SQL statement, regardless of the actual ON CONFLICT mode specified.

method.name: vtab_innocuous
method.description: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_INNOCUOUS) from within the xConnect or xCreate methods of a virtual table implementation identify that virtual table as being safe to use from within triggers and views. Conceptually, the SQLITE_VTAB_INNOCUOUS tag means that the virtual table can do no serious harm even if it is controlled by a malicious hacker. Developers should avoid setting the SQLITE_VTAB_INNOCUOUS flag unless absolutely necessary.

method.name: vtab_directonly
method.description: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_DIRECTONLY) from within the xConnect or xCreate methods of a virtual table implementation prohibits that virtual table from being used from within triggers and views.

method.name: vtab_uses_all_schemas
method.description: Calls of the form sqlite3_vtab_config(db,SQLITE_VTAB_USES_ALL_SCHEMA) from within the xConnect or xCreate methods of a virtual table implementation instruct the query planner to begin at least a read transaction on all schemas ("main", "temp", and any ATTACH-ed databases) whenever the virtual table is used.

method.name: vtab_nochange
method.description: If the sqlite3_vtab_nochange(X) routine is called within the xColumn method of a virtual table, then it might return true if the column is being fetched as part of an UPDATE operation during which the column value will not change. The virtual table implementation can use this hint as permission to substitute a return value that is less expensive to compute and that the corresponding xUpdate method understands as a "no-change" value.

method.name: vtab_collation
method.description: This function may only be called from within a call to the xBestIndex method of a virtual table. This function returns a pointer to a string that is the name of the appropriate collation sequence to use for text comparisons on the constraint identified by its arguments. The first parameter must be the pointer to the sqlite3_index_info object that is the first parameter to the xBestIndex() method. The second argument must be an index into the aConstraint[] array belonging to the sqlite3_index_info structure passed to xBestIndex. The return value is computed as follows: (1) If the constraint comes from a WHERE clause expression that contains a COLLATE operator, then the name of the collation specified by that COLLATE operator is returned. (2) If there is no COLLATE operator, but the column that is the subject of the constraint specifies an alternative collating sequence via a COLLATE clause on the column definition within the CREATE TABLE statement that was passed into sqlite3_declare_vtab(), then the name of that alternative collating sequence is returned. (3) Otherwise, "BINARY" is returned.

method.name: vtab_rhs_value
method.description: This API may only be used from within the xBestIndex method of a virtual table implementation. The result of calling this interface from outside of an xBestIndex method are undefined and probably harmful. This routine attempts to set out_value to the value of the right-hand operand of the constraint if the right-hand operand is known. Returns SQLITE_OK if *out_value is set to a value, SQLITE_NOTFOUND if the right-hand side of the constraint is not available, or other result codes if something goes wrong. The _rhs_ in the name is an abbreviation for "Right-Hand Side". The returned sqlite3_value object is protected and remains valid for the duration of the xBestIndex method call, and is automatically deallocated when xBestIndex returns.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_v2
method.description: This function returns information about the predicted and measured performance for pStmt. The flags parameter controls which query plan elements are available: if SQLITE_SCANSTAT_COMPLEX is specified, all elements are available; otherwise, only query loop elements are available. Note: This is a simplified wrapper. The actual function writes to a void* pointer with type depending on scan_status_op. For production use, implement operation-specific wrappers with appropriate type handling.

method.name: stmt_scanstatus_reset
method.description: Zero all sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus() related event counters. This API is only available if the library is built with pre-processor symbol SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS defined.

method.name: db_cacheflush
method.description: If a write-transaction is open on the database connection, any dirty pages in the pager-cache that are not currently in use are written out to disk. Flushes caches for all schemas (main, temp, and attached databases). Returns SQLITE_OK if no error, SQLITE_BUSY if locks cannot be obtained and no other error occurs, or other SQLite error codes if other errors occur. This function does not set the database handle error code or message returned by sqlite3_errcode() and sqlite3_errmsg().

method.name: preupdate_hook
method.description: Registers a callback function that is invoked prior to each INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operation on a database table. The callback receives operation type, database name, table name, old rowid, new rowid, old values array, and new values array. This function is only available if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK.

method.name: preupdate_count
method.description: Returns the number of columns in the row that is being inserted, updated, or deleted. May only be called from within a preupdate callback.

method.name: preupdate_depth
method.description: Returns 0 if the preupdate callback was invoked as a result of a direct insert, update, or delete operation; or 1 for triggers invoked by top-level triggers; and so forth. May only be called from within a preupdate callback.

method.name: preupdate_old
method.description: Returns the value of the Nth column of the table row before it is updated. May only be called from within a preupdate callback and only for UPDATE or DELETE operations.

method.name: preupdate_new
method.description: Returns the value of the Nth column of the table row after it is updated. May only be called from within a preupdate callback and only for INSERT or UPDATE operations.

method.name: preupdate_blobwrite
method.description: Returns the index of the column being written when sqlite3_blob_write() is used to update a blob column, or -1 for other operations. May only be called from within a preupdate callback.

method.name: system_errno
method.description: Returns the underlying operating system error code or error number that caused the most recent I/O error or failure to open a file. The return value is OS-dependent (e.g., on Unix systems, this returns the errno value). This function is only meaningful after an I/O error occurs, such as when sqlite3_open_v2() returns SQLITE_CANTOPEN.

method.name: serialize
method.description: Serializes the database to a byte array. Returns a PackedByteArray containing the database contents. Memory is copied and managed by Godot. The flags argument can include SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY to avoid allocation (but the data is still copied by this wrapper for safety). Use .size() on the result to get the size.

method.name: serialize_flag_nocopy
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_serialize() to avoid memory allocation by returning a pointer to existing contiguous memory.

method.name: deserialize_flag_freeonclose
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize(): SQLite takes ownership of the serialization buffer and frees it on close.

method.name: deserialize_flag_resizeable
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize(): SQLite is allowed to grow the database size using sqlite3_realloc64().

method.name: deserialize_flag_readonly
method.description: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize(): The deserialized database is treated as read-only.

method.name: carray_bind
method.description: Binds an array value to a parameter of a prepared statement for use with the carray() table-valued function. Uses no destructor (SQLITE_STATIC behavior). Advanced use cases requiring custom destructors should use the raw C API.

method.name: carray_type_int32
method.description: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): 32-bit signed integers.

method.name: carray_type_int64
method.description: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): 64-bit signed integers.

method.name: carray_type_double
method.description: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): doubles.

method.name: carray_type_text
method.description: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): char* strings.

method.name: carray_type_blob
method.description: Datatype flag for sqlite3_carray_bind(): struct iovec (binary data).

method.name: session_delete
method.description: Delete a session object previously allocated using sqlite3session_create. Once deleted, using the session object with any other session module function results in undefined behavior.

method.name: mutex_alloc
method.description: Allocates a new mutex. The type parameter must be one of the SQLITE_MUTEX_* constants. Dynamic mutexes (SQLITE_MUTEX_FAST and SQLITE_MUTEX_RECURSIVE) create new mutexes on each call, while static mutexes return the same mutex for the same type number.

method.name: str_appendf
method.description: Appends formatted text to the dynamic string using SQLite's printf functionality. The format string uses SQLite's printf formatting rules.

method.name: str_errcode
method.description: Returns the current error code of the sqlite3_str object. Returns SQLITE_NOMEM for out-of-memory errors, SQLITE_TOOBIG if the size exceeds SQLITE_MAX_LENGTH, or SQLITE_OK if there are no errors.

method.name: status
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information about SQLite's performance. Returns a Dictionary with 'current' and 'highwater' keys. Returns an empty Dictionary on error.

method.name: snapshot_open
method.description: Starts a read transaction on an historical snapshot. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: snapshot_cmp
method.description: Compares the ages of two snapshot handles. Returns negative if p1 is older, zero if same, positive if p1 is newer.

method.name: snapshot_recover
method.description: Recovers valid snapshots from a WAL file so they can be used with sqlite3_snapshot_open(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: session_object_config
method.description: Configure a session object after creation. Valid op codes include SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE and SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_ROWID.

method.name: session_objconfig_size
method.description: Option for sqlite3session_object_config to configure the sqlite3session_changeset_size() API.

method.name: session_objconfig_rowid
method.description: Option for sqlite3session_object_config to enable collection of data for tables with no explicit PRIMARY KEY.

method.name: session_indirect
method.description: Set, clear, or query the session object's indirect flag. Passing zero clears the flag, greater than zero sets it, and less than zero queries the current state without modifying it. Returns 1 if the indirect flag is set, 0 if clear.

method.name: session_attach
method.description: Attach a table to the session object. If z_tab is empty or null, changes are recorded for all tables. Returns SQLITE_OK on success.

method.name: session_table_filter
method.description: Set a table filter callback on the session object. The filter callback receives the table name and returns 0 to skip tracking or non-zero to track changes. Note that once a table is attached, the filter will not be called again for it.

method.name: session_changeset
method.description: Obtain a changeset containing changes to attached tables. On success, out_changeset contains the changeset buffer and out_size its size in bytes. The caller is responsible for freeing the buffer via sqlite3_free (handled internally here). Returns SQLITE_OK on success.

method.name: session_changeset_size
method.description: Return an upper-limit for the size of the changeset in bytes. Only meaningful if SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE was enabled via sqlite3session_object_config(). Returns 0 if disabled or no changes.

method.name: session_diff
method.description: Load the difference between tables into a session. Compares the table attached to the session with a compatible table in another database and adds INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE records. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. On error, out_error contains the error message.

method.name: session_patchset
method.description: Generate a patchset from a session object. A patchset is a compact version of a changeset with reduced field data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. out_patchset contains the patchset buffer and out_size its size in bytes.

method.name: session_isempty
method.description: Return true if no changes have been recorded by the session object. If one or more changes have been recorded, return false. If this function returns true, a subsequent call to session_changeset() is guaranteed to return an empty changeset.

method.name: changeset_start
method.description: Create an iterator to traverse a changeset. On success, out_iter contains the iterator handle. The changeset buffer must remain valid until the iterator is destroyed.

method.name: changeset_start_invert_flag
method.description: Flag for sqlite3changeset_start_v2 to invert the changeset while iterating. It is an error to specify this flag with a patchset.

method.name: changeset_op
method.description: Obtain the current operation from a changeset iterator. On success, out_table contains the table name, out_col_count the column count, out_op the operation type (SQLITE_INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE), and out_indirect whether the change is indirect.

method.name: changeset_pk
method.description: Obtain the primary key definition of the table modified by the current change. On success, out_pk contains a boolean array (1 for PK columns, 0 otherwise) and out_col_count the number of columns.

method.name: changeset_old
method.description: Obtain an old.* value from a changeset iterator. For DELETE or UPDATE changes, returns the i_val-th original column value. On success, out_value contains the sqlite3_value handle.

method.name: changeset_new
method.description: Obtain a new.* value from a changeset iterator. For UPDATE or INSERT changes, returns the i_val-th new column value. If the change does not include a new value for the column, out_value is set to null. On success, out_value contains the sqlite3_value handle.

method.name: changeset_conflict
method.description: Obtain a conflicting row value from a changeset iterator. For UPDATE or INSERT changes, returns the i_val-th value from the conflicting row. Only valid for iterators passed to conflict-handler callbacks with SQLITE_CHANGESET_DATA or SQLITE_CHANGESET_CONFLICT. Returns SQLITE_OK on success and sets out_value to the conflicting value.

method.name: changeset_fk_conflicts
method.description: Determine the number of foreign key constraint violations. May only be called with an iterator passed to an SQLITE_CHANGESET_FOREIGN_KEY conflict handler callback. Returns an Array with [error_code, fk_count]. On success, error_code is SQLITE_OK and fk_count is the total number of known foreign key violations.

method.name: changeset_finalize
method.description: Finalize a changeset iterator allocated with sqlite3changeset_start(). Should only be called on iterators created using sqlite3changeset_start(). If called on iterators passed to conflict handlers, returns SQLITE_MISUSE immediately.

method.name: changeset_invert
method.description: Invert a changeset. Reverses the effects of applying the uninverted changeset: DELETE changes become INSERT, INSERT become DELETE, and UPDATE old/new values are exchanged. Returns an Array with [error_code, inverted_changeset]. The caller is responsible for memory management of the output buffer via SQLite's sqlite3_free().

method.name: changegroup_schema
method.description: Add a schema to a changegroup to enforce compatibility with database zDb. If sqlite3changegroup_add() is called with changesets that are not compatible with the configured schema, SQLITE_SCHEMA is returned and the changegroup object is left in an undefined state.

method.name: changegroup_add
method.description: Add all changes within the changeset (or patchset) in buffer input to the changegroup. All prior calls to this function on the same changegroup object must also have specified patchsets (or all changesets). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code if an error occurs.

method.name: changegroup_add_change
method.description: Add the single change currently indicated by the iterator to the changegroup. The iterator must point to a valid entry and must not have been opened with the SQLITE_CHANGESETAPPLY_INVERT flag. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an SQLite error code otherwise.

method.name: changegroup_output
method.description: Obtain a buffer containing a changeset (or patchset) representing the current contents of the changegroup. Returns an Array with [error_code, output_changeset]. The caller is responsible for memory management of the output buffer via sqlite3_free().

method.name: changeset_apply
method.description: Apply a changeset to a database. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional, pass empty Callable for all tables). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: changeset_apply_v2
method.description: Apply a changeset to a database with additional control via flags. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional, pass empty Callable for all tables). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred and the changeset is not a patchset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: changeset_apply_v3
method.description: Apply a changeset to a database with v3 semantics. The filter_callback receives an iterator handle for the current change instead of just a table name, allowing more sophisticated filtering. The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred and the changeset is not a patchset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: changeset_apply_flag_no_savepoint
method.description: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Do not enclose operations in a savepoint.

method.name: changeset_apply_flag_invert
method.description: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Invert the changeset before applying it.

method.name: changeset_apply_flag_ignore_noop
method.description: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Do not invoke conflict handler for changes that would not modify the database.

method.name: changeset_apply_flag_fk_no_action
method.description: Flag for sqlite3changeset_apply_v2/v3: Foreign keys behave as if declared with ON UPDATE/DELETE NO ACTION.

method.name: changeset_conflict_type_data
method.description: Conflict type: Non-primary key fields don't contain expected 'before' values.

method.name: changeset_conflict_type_not_found
method.description: Conflict type: Row with required primary key fields not present in database.

method.name: changeset_conflict_type_conflict
method.description: Conflict type: INSERT would result in duplicate primary key values.

method.name: changeset_conflict_type_constraint
method.description: Conflict type: Other constraint violation (UNIQUE, CHECK, NOT NULL).

method.name: changeset_conflict_type_foreign_key
method.description: Conflict type: Foreign key constraint violation after applying changeset.

method.name: changeset_conflict_result_omit
method.description: Conflict handler return value: Skip the conflicting change.

method.name: changeset_conflict_result_replace
method.description: Conflict handler return value: Replace the conflicting row (only for DATA or CONFLICT conflicts).

method.name: changeset_conflict_result_abort
method.description: Conflict handler return value: Roll back all changes and abort.

method.name: rebaser_rebase
method.description: Rebases a changeset using the configured rebase data. Returns the rebased changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: changegroup_output_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changegroup_output. Extracts a changeset from a changegroup. Returns the changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: changeset_apply_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_apply. Takes a PackedByteArray for the changeset and applies it to the database. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: changeset_apply_v2_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_apply_v2. Takes a PackedByteArray for the changeset and applies it to the database. The filter_callback determines which tables to apply changes to (optional). The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: changeset_apply_v3_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_apply_v3. Takes a PackedByteArray for the changeset and applies it to the database. The filter_callback receives an iterator handle for the current change instead of just a table name, allowing more sophisticated filtering. The conflict_callback handles conflicts during application (required). The rebase_data output parameter receives rebased data if conflicts occurred. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

method.name: changeset_invert_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_invert. Inverts a changeset (converts INSERTs to DELETEs and vice versa, swaps UPDATE old/new values). Returns the inverted changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: changeset_start_stream
method.description: Streaming version of changeset_start. Starts iterating over a changeset. Returns a Ref to the iterator handle, or an empty Ref on error.

method.name: session_config_stream_size
method.description: Configures the streaming data chunk size for the sessions module. Returns the final chunk size on success, or an SQLite error code on failure. Note: This function is not threadsafe and must not be called while any other thread is inside sessions methods or after any sessions objects have been created.

method.name: rebaser_rebase_stream
method.description: Streaming version of rebaser_rebase. Rebases a changeset using the rebaser. Returns the rebased changeset as a PackedByteArray, or an empty array on error.

method.name: fts5_tokenize_query
method.description: Flag indicating tokenization is for a query.

method.name: fts5_tokenize_prefix
method.description: Flag indicating the last token is a prefix.

method.name: fts5_tokenize_document
method.description: Flag indicating tokenization is for a document.

method.name: fts5_tokenize_aux
method.description: Flag indicating tokenization is for an auxiliary function.

method.name: fts5_token_colocated
method.description: Flag indicating the token is a synonym of the previous token.

method.name: create_module_v2
method.description: Registers a new virtual table module name with a destructor callback. The destructor is called when SQLite no longer needs the client data. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

method.name: unlock_notify
method.description: Registers an unlock-notify callback that SQLite will invoke when the blocking connection relinquishes its locks. The callback receives the user_data as its argument. Only available if compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_UNLOCK_NOTIFY.

method.name: declare_vtab
method.description: Declares the schema of a virtual table. The SQL statement should be a CREATE TABLE statement that defines the column names and types.

method.name: drop_modules
method.description: Removes virtual table modules from the database connection, keeping only those named in the keep array. If keep is empty, all modules are removed. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code otherwise.

method.name: vtab_distinct
method.description: This API may only be used from within an xBestIndex method of a virtual table implementation. The result of calling this interface from outside of xBestIndex() is undefined and probably harmful. ^The sqlite3_vtab_distinct() interface returns an integer between 0 and 3. The integer returned gives the virtual table additional information about how the query planner wants the output to be ordered. Based on the return value, the virtual table may set the "orderByConsumed" flag if it can meet the ordering requirements. Return value 0: rows must be returned in sorted order as defined by nOrderBy and aOrderBy fields. Return value 1: rows don't need to be in sorted order but duplicates must be adjacent (GROUP BY). Return value 2: rows don't need to be in order but duplicates may be omitted (DISTINCT). Return value 3: rows must be in order and duplicates may be omitted (DISTINCT with ORDER BY).

method.name: conflict_rollback
method.description: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT ROLLBACK mode.

method.name: conflict_ignore
method.description: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT IGNORE mode. Also used by sqlite3_set_authorizer() callback.

method.name: conflict_fail
method.description: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT FAIL mode.

method.name: conflict_replace
method.description: Returned by sqlite3_vtab_on_conflict() to indicate ON CONFLICT REPLACE mode.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_nloop
method.description: The sqlite3_int64 variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the total number of times that the X-th loop has run.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_nvisit
method.description: The sqlite3_int64 variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the total number of rows examined by all iterations of the X-th loop.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_est
method.description: The "double" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the query planner's estimate for the average number of rows output from each iteration of the X-th loop.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_name
method.description: The "const char *" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to a zero-terminated UTF-8 string containing the name of the index or table used for the X-th loop.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_explain
method.description: The "const char *" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to a zero-terminated UTF-8 string containing the EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN description for the X-th loop.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_selectid
method.description: The "int" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the id for the X-th query plan element. The id value is unique within the statement.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_parentid
method.description: The "int" variable pointed to by the V parameter will be set to the id of the parent of the current query element, if applicable, or to zero if the query element has no parent.

method.name: stmt_scanstat_ncycle
method.description: The sqlite3_int64 output value is set to the number of cycles, according to the processor time-stamp counter, that elapsed while the query element was being processed.

method.name: vtab_in
method.description: This interface may only be used from within an xBestIndex() method of a virtual table implementation. The result of invoking this interface from any other context is undefined and probably harmful. A constraint on a virtual table of the form [IN operator] is communicated to the xBestIndex method as a SQLITE_INDEX_CONSTRAINT_EQ constraint. The bHandle parameter has three modes: (1) bHandle == -1: query mode - returns true if the constraint can be processed all-at-once; (2) bHandle == 0: tell SQLite not to use all-at-once processing; (3) bHandle >= 1: tell SQLite to use all-at-once processing. For all-at-once processing to be selected, both conditions must be met: (1) aConstraintUsage[].argvIndex is set to a positive integer, and (2) the last call with non-negative bHandle had bHandle >= 1.

method.name: vtab_in_first
method.description: These interfaces are only useful from within the xFilter() method of a virtual table implementation. The result of invoking these interfaces from any other context is undefined and probably harmful. Use these routines to access all values on the right-hand side of the IN constraint. On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_value to point to the first value on the RHS of the IN constraint. If there are no more values, out_value is set to NULL and returns SQLITE_DONE. The return value might be some other value, such as SQLITE_NOMEM, in the event of a malfunction.
